prompt: Should Reveal charge for community access in v1?
method: constitution-bound expand simulation (same packs/contracts/evidence as forge expand-node; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unavailable in eval VM)

nodes.label: Promise before price
nodes.details: v1 access is part of the founding story — belonging before tools, trust before transactions.

nodes.label: What paid would mean
nodes.details: Paid later must mean deeper formation or stewardship, never buying the right to be safe.

nodes.label: Exclusion hollows wedge
nodes.details: Charging at the door recreates the exclusion traditional finance culture already taught her.

nodes.label: Later as stewardship
nodes.details: If money enters, frame it as sustaining the house she already belongs in — not a ticket.

nodes.label: Reject trivial paywall
nodes.details: Even if entitlements are easy to code, Storyteller rejects v1 paywall as a meaning failure.
nodes.strategicAnswer: Don't invent a paywall that breaks trust.
